skill.name: maxhub-skills
skill.description: MaxHub Skills is a multi-platform API data query aggregation skill covering 20 platforms including Bilibili, Douyin, Instagram, YouTube, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Reddit, Xiaohongshu, Weibo, Zhihu, and more. Supports unified data queries across social media, short video, professional networking, and email verification services via a single API key. / MaxHub 技能仓库聚合入口，涵盖20个平台的数据查询技能，覆盖国内外主流社交媒体、短视频、职场、邮件等全场景API数据服务。
skill.summary: Multi-platform data query toolkit for 20 social media & productivity APIs. Query user profiles, trending content, videos, and more across Bilibili, Douyin, Instagram, YouTube, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Reddit, and 13 other platforms. / 覆盖20个平台的统一数据查询入口，支持国内外主流社交媒体与职场平台的API数据服务。
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation-only aggregation of API reference files for 20 social media and productivity platforms. The static analysis flagged 13,898 potential issues across 161 files (47,956 lines), but 100% of these are false positives resulting from the skill's documentation-based nature. The 9,304 'external_commands' flags are curl command examples embedded in Markdown reference docs. The 825 'network' flags are documented API endpoint URLs for the MaxHub service (https://www.aconfig.cn). The 350 'env_access' flags all reference the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable, which is a documented dependency. No executable scripts (shell, Python, Ruby, JS) exist anywhere in the repository — all files are pure Markdown and JSON metadata. The skill requires network access to one known API endpoint and a user-provided API key, which is appropriate for its stated function. Risk level is LOW: the skill operates as documented with no hidden or malicious behaviors.

medium_findings.title: Documented curl command examples in markdown files
medium_findings.description: 9,304 instances of curl commands found throughout reference documentation and README files across all 20 platform subdirectories. These are API usage examples in Markdown code blocks as part of the skill's documentation. While the skill does declare 'curl' as a required binary dependency and the ClawHub runtime may execute curl commands, the commands are documented API calls to https://www.aconfig.cn, not arbitrary command execution. Confidence: TRUE POSITIVE (the commands exist but are legitimate documentation of the skill's API interface).

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded API endpoint URLs in documentation
medium_findings.description: 825 instances of hardcoded URLs pointing to https://www.aconfig.cn found across all platform reference files, READMEs, and SKILL.md files. These URLs are the legitimate API endpoints for the MaxHub service. Every endpoint is documented with its HTTP method, path, parameters, and response format. This is expected behavior for an API integration skill. Confidence: TRUE POSITIVE (URLs are real API endpoints intentionally documented).

low_findings.title: MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable references
low_findings.description: 350 instances of API key/secret references across documentation files. The skill requires MAXHUB_API_KEY as a documented dependency. These references appear in SKILL.md dependency declarations, README quick-start instructions, and API examples. The key is used for Bearer token authentication with the MaxHub API. No actual credential values are embedded in the repository. Confidence: TRUE POSITIVE (env var usage is legitimate and documented).

low_findings.title: Potential obfuscation patterns in documentation (high entropy / long lines)
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged high-entropy strings and long lines primarily in API reference markdown files. These are API response examples, request parameter tables, and JSON response schemas embedded in documentation — not obfuscated code. Confidence: FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged potential weak crypto patterns. These are false positives triggered by API key format patterns (Bearer token strings) and URL parameter signatures in API documentation. No actual cryptographic code exists in this documentation-only repository. Confidence: FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: C2 keyword detection in API documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged potential C2 (command & control) keywords in API search reference files. These keywords are legitimate API search endpoint parameter names (e.g., search terms, pagination parameters), not C2 indicators. Confidence: FALSE POSITIVE.

dangerous_patterns.title: Combination of external commands, network access, and credential access documented
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analysis heuristic flagged a DANGEROUS COMBINATION of code_execution + network + credential_access risk factors. In this context, the 'code_execution' is curl command examples in documentation, the 'network' is documented API URLs, and 'credential_access' is the MAXHUB_API_KEY env var. While this combination sounds alarming, the skill contains no executable scripts and all components are legitimate, documented features of an API integration skill. The ClawHub runtime reads the documentation and executes curl commands against the MaxHub API using the user-provided key. Confidence: FALSE POSITIVE — this pattern is expected and appropriate for the skill's declared purpose.

content.user_title: MaxHub Multi-Platform Data Query Toolkit — 20 Social Media & Productivity APIs in One Skill
content.value_statement: Eliminates the need to learn 20 different platform APIs. MaxHub Skills provides a unified, AI-accessible interface for querying data across Bilibili, Douyin (TikTok China), Instagram, YouTube, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Reddit, Xiaohongshu, Weibo, Zhihu, and 11 more platforms — all through a single API key and consistent endpoint patterns.
actual_capabilities: Query user profile information across 20 social media and productivity platforms
Search for content, videos, posts, and articles using platform-specific search APIs
Retrieve trending/hot content across Bilibili, Douyin, Weibo, Zhihu, and more
Fetch video details, comments, and metadata from YouTube, Bilibili, Douyin, Kuaishou, and Xigua
Access LinkedIn company information, job listings, and content feeds
Query Instagram post data, user profiles, and search content
Retrieve Reddit posts, comments, and subreddit information
Access Twitter/X tweet data and user timelines
Query Xiaohongshu (RED) notes, product topics, and user profiles
Fetch email verification data via temp-mail integration
Access Zhihu content, trending topics, and user profiles
Parameter mapping system for consistent query patterns across all platforms
Bilingual support (Chinese and English) for platform-specific API responses
Unified authentication via single MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable
limitations: Requires an active MAXHUB_API_KEY obtained from https://www.aconfig.cn
Network access to https://www.aconfig.cn is required — will not work in air-gapped environments
Depends on curl binary availability in the execution environment
Limited to data query operations only — no content posting, editing, or deletion capabilities
API rate limits apply per platform — max 5 pages, 50 items per request
Chain depth limited to 3 levels, batch size limited to 10 items
Requires platform-specific parameter knowledge documented in reference files
Data freshness depends on the upstream MaxHub API update frequency
Not all endpoints support both Chinese and English response languages

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Research
use_cases.description: A content marketer needs to research trending topics across multiple Chinese and international social platforms. Using MaxHub Skills, they can query Bilibili trending videos, Douyin hot searches, Weibo trending topics, and Zhihu hot content — all within a single conversation, without switching between different API documentations.
use_cases.target_user: Content marketers, social media managers, trend analysts

use_cases.title: Multi-Platform User Profile Aggregation
use_cases.description: A digital investigator or recruiter needs to gather publicly available profile information for a target across multiple platforms. MaxHub Skills enables querying user profiles on Bilibili, Douyin, Instagram, LinkedIn, GitHub, Reddit, Twitter, and more through consistent API patterns, aggregating results in one place.

use_cases.title: Cross-Platform Video Performance Analysis
use_cases.description: A video creator publishes content across YouTube, Bilibili, Douyin, and Kuaishou. They can use MaxHub Skills to query video statistics (views, likes, comments) from each platform simultaneously, compare performance metrics, and identify which content resonates best on each platform.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators, video marketers, multi-platform publishers

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Platform Trending Content Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the MaxHub Skills to find trending content across Bilibili, Douyin, and Weibo. Query each platform's trending/hot endpoints and compile a summary of the top 5 trending items per platform with their titles, engagement metrics, and platform-specific context.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cross-platform trend research for content strategy planning

prompt_templates.title: Single User Cross-Platform Profile Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to find information about a user across multiple platforms. Using MaxHub Skills, search for user profiles with the username '{username}' on Instagram, Twitter, Reddit, GitHub, and LinkedIn. For each platform found, return the display name, bio/description, follower count, and join date.
prompt_templates.scenario: Digital identity research or competitive analysis

prompt_templates.title: Video Performance Benchmarking
prompt_templates.prompt: Using MaxHub Skills, fetch video details for the following IDs: Bilibivi BV {id}, YouTube video {id}, and Douyin video {id}. Compare their view counts, like counts, comment counts, and publish dates. Present the data in a comparison table and identify which platform's content is performing best.

prompt_templates.title: Platform-Specific Search and Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Using MaxHub Skills, search for '{query}' on Xiaohongshu, Zhihu, and Douyin. For Xiaohongshu, look for notes and product topics. For Zhihu, find relevant questions and answers. For Douyin, find related videos. Summarize the top results from each platform with brief descriptions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive topic research across Chinese social platforms

output_examples.input: Find trending content on Bilibili and Douyin
output_examples.output:
## Bilibili Trending (Top 3)
1. [Video Title] - 2.3M views, 45K likes
2. [Video Title] - 1.8M views, 32K likes
3. [Video Title] - 1.2M views, 28K likes

## Douyin Hot Search (Top 3)
1. [Topic] - 15.6M热度
2. [Topic] - 12.3M热度
3. [Topic] - 9.8M热度

output_examples.input: Get user profile for 'example_user' on Instagram and Twitter

best_practices: Always verify the platform-specific parameter mappings before making complex queries — each platform has unique parameter names and constraints
Start with simple single-platform queries to validate your API key and network connectivity before attempting multi-platform aggregation
Use the reference documentation files within each platform subdirectory to understand available endpoints, required parameters, and response formats
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to post, edit, or delete content — MaxHub Skills only supports data query operations and lacks write permissions
Avoid making excessive parallel API calls — respect the documented rate limits (max 5 pages, batch size 10) to prevent throttling
Do not hardcode the MAXHUB_API_KEY into prompts or share it with others — always use the environment variable as documented

faq.question: What is MaxHub Skills and what platforms does it support?
faq.answer: MaxHub Skills is an aggregation skill that provides unified API data query access to 20 platforms: Bilibili, Douyin (TikTok China), Instagram, Kuaishou, Lemon8, LinkedIn, Pipixia, Reddit, Sora2, Temp Mail, Threads, TikTok, Toutiao, Twitter/X, WeChat, Weibo, Xiaohongshu, Xigua, YouTube, and Zhihu.

faq.question: How do I get started with MaxHub Skills?
faq.answer: First, obtain a MAXHUB_API_KEY from https://www.aconfig.cn. Set it as an environment variable in your AI tool. Then install the skill via your marketplace. The skill will automatically use the API key for authentication. Ensure curl is available in your environment.

faq.question: Is MaxHub Skills safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is documentation-only (no executable scripts). It makes HTTPS requests exclusively to https://www.aconfig.cn. Your API key is passed via environment variable and never stored or shared. The skill only supports read/query operations — no content modification or deletion.

faq.question: What are the API rate limits?
faq.answer: MaxHub enforces the following limits: maximum 5 pages per query, 50 items per page, chain depth of 3 levels, and batch size of 10 items. Different platforms may have additional platform-specific restrictions documented in the reference files.

faq.question: Does MaxHub Skills work in both Chinese and English?
faq.answer: Yes. All platforms support queries in Chinese and English. However, individual API endpoints may have language-specific response formats. The documentation specifies which language each endpoint uses. Most Chinese platforms (Bilibili, Douyin, Weibo, etc.) return Chinese responses by default.

faq.question: What dependencies does MaxHub Skills require?
faq.answer: MaxHub Skills requires: (1) MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable for authentication, (2) curl binary for HTTP requests, (3) network access to https://www.aconfig.cn, and (4) a compatible AI tool (Claude, Codex, or Claude Code) that supports the ClawHub skill runtime.
